title: 30-Day Social Content Calendar
author: PromptDeck
description: Builds a full month of platform-specific social posts with hooks, formats, and CTAs.
prompt:
Role: You are a social media manager and content strategist.

Task: Create a 30-day content calendar for [BRAND/NICHE] on [PLATFORM] with the goal [GROW FOLLOWERS/DRIVE SALES] in brand voice [VOICE]. Rotate 4 pillars — educational, entertaining, promotional, community — and front-load the strongest hooks.

Format: A markdown table — Day | Pillar | Post Format | Hook | Caption Idea | CTA | Hashtags. After the table, suggest 3 recurring series to build consistency.

title: Detailed Buyer Persona Generator
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates a rich buyer persona with goals, pains, objections, and messaging angles.
prompt:
Role: You are a marketing strategist and customer researcher.

Task: Create a detailed buyer persona for a business selling [PRODUCT/SERVICE] to [TARGET MARKET]. Base it on realistic assumptions and label anything speculative.

Format: A structured profile — Name, age, role, one-line bio; Demographics/firmographics; Goals & motivations; Pains & frustrations; Buying triggers & decision criteria; Common objections (with counters); Preferred channels; 3 messaging angles each with a sample one-liner.

title: YouTube Video Script Creator
author: PromptDeck
description: Creates a retention-optimized YouTube script with hook, chapters, B-roll notes, and CTA.
prompt:
Role: You are a YouTube growth strategist and scriptwriter.

Task: Write a [MINUTES]-minute script about [VIDEO TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE], using retention tactics: open loops, pattern interrupts, callbacks, and mini-cliffhangers. Spoken, energetic tone with no filler.

Format: (1) 3 title options + a thumbnail text idea; (2) a 10-second hook; (3) a chaptered script with timestamps and inline [B-ROLL] and [ON-SCREEN TEXT] notes; (4) an end-screen CTA and pinned-comment suggestion.

title: Brand Voice & Messaging Guide
author: PromptDeck
description: Defines a brand's voice, tone, vocabulary, and do/don't rules for consistent content.
prompt:
Role: You are a brand strategist and voice designer.

Task: Create a brand voice guide for [BRAND], selling [WHAT] to [AUDIENCE], wanting to feel [3 ADJECTIVES].

Format: (1) 3 core voice traits, each with a definition and a 'we sound like / we don't sound like' pair; (2) tone shifts by context (sales vs support vs social); (3) vocabulary we use / avoid; (4) 2 sample paragraphs of the same message in-voice; (5) a 5-point pre-publish checklist.

title: Ad Copy Variations (Meta / Google)
author: PromptDeck
description: Produces multiple ad-copy variations with different angles for A/B testing.
prompt:
Role: You are a performance marketing copywriter.

Task: Write ad copy for [PRODUCT/SERVICE] targeting [AUDIENCE] on [PLATFORM] with goal [CONVERSIONS/CLICKS/LEADS], key benefit [BENEFIT], offer [OFFER]. Create 5 variations with different angles: pain, desire, social proof, curiosity, urgency.

Format: For each variation — primary text (≤125 chars where relevant), headline (≤40 chars), description. Add 3 CTA button ideas. Note which angle to test first and why.

title: Professional Press Release
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a newsworthy, properly formatted press release with quotes and boilerplate.
prompt:
Role: You are a PR writer for a communications agency.

Task: Write a press release announcing [WHAT'S HAPPENING] for [COMPANY] in [CITY] on [DATE], with key facts [FACTS] and news angle [ANGLE]. Keep it factual and news-style, not salesy.

Format: Standard format — compelling headline + subhead; dateline; a lead answering who/what/when/where/why; supporting details; 1–2 labeled quotes; an 'About' boilerplate; a media-contact placeholder; end with ###.

title: Email Newsletter Issue
author: PromptDeck
description: Drafts a complete, engaging newsletter issue with a hook and a single CTA.
prompt:
Role: You are an email newsletter writer with a strong personal voice.

Task: Write a newsletter issue for [BRAND/NEWSLETTER], audience [WHO], about [THEME], in voice [VOICE], around 400–600 words with no fluff.

Format: (1) 3 subject line options + preview text; (2) a personal-feeling intro hook; (3) 2–3 short value sections with subheads; (4) one curated 'worth checking out' placeholder; (5) a single clear CTA.

title: Instagram Carousel Script
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a slide-by-slide carousel with a scroll-stopping cover and save-worthy payoff.
prompt:
Role: You are a short-form content designer.

Task: Create a [7–10]-slide carousel for [PLATFORM] on [TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE]. Slide 1 is a bold hook; middle slides carry one concrete idea each; second-to-last recaps; last is a CTA (follow/save/comment). Make it genuinely save-worthy.

Format: For each slide — on-slide text (≤2 lines) + a tiny design note. Then the post caption (hook + context + CTA) and 5 suggested hashtags.

title: Short-Form Video Hooks (Reels/TikTok)
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates scroll-stopping first-3-second hooks plus a short script skeleton.
prompt:
Role: You are a short-form video strategist.

Task: Generate 15 scroll-stopping hooks for a video about [TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE], mixing formats (bold claim, question, mistake, 'stop doing X', curiosity gap, result tease), each under 12 words. Then script the best one.

Format: The 15 hooks as a list, then a 20–35 second script skeleton for the best hook (hook → 3 value beats → payoff → CTA) with [B-ROLL] and [ON-SCREEN TEXT] notes.

title: Podcast Episode Outline & Questions
author: PromptDeck
description: Plans a podcast episode with a hook, segment flow, and sharp interview questions.
prompt:
Role: You are a podcast producer and interviewer.

Task: Plan an episode about [TOPIC] for [AUDIENCE], format [SOLO/INTERVIEW with GUEST ROLE]. Make it conversational, not a lecture.

Format: (1) 3 title options + a 2-sentence hook; (2) a segment-by-segment outline with rough timings; (3) if interview, 10 non-obvious questions ordered to build a narrative plus 3 follow-ups; (4) 3 quotable teasers for social; (5) a closing CTA.

title: Customer Case Study / Success Story
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns raw results into a persuasive challenge-solution-results case study.
prompt:
Role: You are a B2B content writer specializing in case studies.

Task: Write a 400–500 word case study for customer [NAME/INDUSTRY], whose challenge was [PROBLEM], who used our product for [USE], achieving [METRICS/OUTCOMES]. Keep it credible and specific — story first, sales second.

Format: A results-led headline; a short customer intro; the Challenge; the Solution; the Results with metrics highlighted; a realistic pull-quote; a soft CTA.

title: Repurpose One Piece Into Ten
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns a single blog, video, or transcript into a full multi-channel content package.
prompt:
Role: You are a content repurposing specialist.

Task: Repurpose the source content below into a multi-channel package for [BRAND/AUDIENCE], adapting each asset to its platform (not copy-paste), in voice [VOICE].

Format: Deliver — 1 LinkedIn post; 1 X thread (6–8 tweets); 3 short-form video hooks + a 20s script for one; 1 Instagram carousel outline; 1 newsletter blurb; 5 pull-quote graphics; 3 SEO title ideas for a spin-off article. Source: [PASTE CONTENT].

title: X / Twitter Thread Writer
author: PromptDeck
description: Turns one idea into a tight, high-engagement thread with a strong hook and CTA close.
prompt:
Role: You are a viral X/Twitter ghostwriter.

Task: Write a thread about [TOPIC/IDEA] for [AUDIENCE] in a [TONE] tone. Tweet 1 promises a specific payoff and creates curiosity (no clickbait lies); each tweet is self-contained, under 280 characters, one idea, skimmable; include one example or mini-story; final tweet summarizes + CTA (follow/bookmark/reply).

Format: 6–10 numbered tweets.

title: Viral LinkedIn Post
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes an engagement-optimized LinkedIn post with a strong hook and discussion CTA.
prompt:
Role: You are a personal-branding ghostwriter for LinkedIn.

Task: Write a post for a [ROLE] about [TOPIC/IDEA] with goal [AUTHORITY/LEADS/DISCUSSION]. Use a scroll-stopping 1-line hook, short mobile-friendly lines, a real story or example, one takeaway, and a comment-inviting question. Avoid buzzwords and 'Agree?' endings.

Format: The post with generous whitespace, no hashtags in the body, and 3 suggested hashtags at the end.

title: Product Launch / Go-To-Market Plan
author: PromptDeck
description: Builds a phased launch plan with channels, messaging, and a timeline.
prompt:
Role: You are a go-to-market strategist.

Task: Create a launch plan for [PRODUCT] aimed at [AUDIENCE] with goal [GOAL] and budget level [BUDGET]. Cover pre-launch, launch, and post-launch.

Format: (1) core positioning & message; (2) target channels with why; (3) a phased timeline table (Phase | Actions | Owner | KPI); (4) a launch-day checklist; (5) 3 risks and mitigations. Keep it actionable.

title: Influencer / Creator Outreach DM
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a personalized outreach message to partner with a creator.
prompt:
Role: You are an influencer marketing manager.

Task: Write outreach to [CREATOR/NICHE] to collaborate on [CAMPAIGN/PRODUCT], with a clear value exchange [WHAT THEY GET]. Personalized, warm, no spam; make the ask easy.

Format: A short DM (≤90 words) with a personalized opener [PLACEHOLDER], one line on why them, the offer, and a low-friction next step. Then a slightly longer email version and 3 subject lines.

title: Campaign Concept & Big Idea
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates creative campaign concepts with a big idea, tagline, and channel plan.
prompt:
Role: You are a creative director.

Task: Develop 3 campaign concepts for [BRAND] to achieve [GOAL] with [AUDIENCE], budget [BUDGET]. Each needs a distinct 'big idea', not just tactics.

Format: For each concept — a name, the big idea in one sentence, the core insight it taps, a tagline, 3 channel activations, and one hero asset idea. End by recommending the strongest and why.

title: SPIN Sales Discovery Questions
author: PromptDeck
description: Generates tailored SPIN discovery questions for a consultative sales call.
prompt:
Role: You are an enterprise sales coach.

Task: Build a discovery-call question set using the SPIN framework for someone selling [PRODUCT/SERVICE] to [BUYER ROLE] at [COMPANY TYPE]. Make questions specific, open-ended, and non-leading.

Format: 3–4 questions each for Situation, Problem, Implication, and Need-payoff. End with 2 questions to uncover budget and decision process without being pushy.

title: Sales Objection Handling Scripts
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes empathetic responses to your most common sales objections.
prompt:
Role: You are a top sales closer.

Task: Write response scripts for my offer [PRODUCT/SERVICE] at [PRICE] handling these objections: 'too expensive', 'need to think about it', 'we already use [competitor]', 'no budget now', 'send me info'. Acknowledge, reframe, and move to a next step without pushiness.

Format: For each objection — a response under 90 words plus one clarifying question to continue the dialogue.

title: Post-Demo Follow-Up Sequence
author: PromptDeck
description: Creates a 4-touch follow-up sequence after a sales call or demo.
prompt:
Role: You are a sales development expert.

Task: Write a 4-message follow-up sequence after a demo of [PRODUCT] to [PROSPECT ROLE] who liked [KEY BENEFIT] but hasn't committed. Helpful and confident, never desperate.

Format: For each message — subject line, body under 120 words, one clear ask. Arc: (1) recap value + resource (same day); (2) address hesitation (+3d); (3) social proof/case study (+5d); (4) polite break-up with an easy yes (+10d).

title: High-Converting B2B Cold Email
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a concise, personalized cold email with one value prop and a low-friction CTA.
prompt:
Role: You are a top B2B cold-email copywriter.

Task: Write a cold email for [WHAT YOU SELL] to [ROLE] at [COMPANY TYPE], whose likely pain is [PAIN POINT], desired action [BOOK A CALL/REPLY]. Under 120 words, personalized first line [PLACEHOLDER], one value proposition, conversational, no 'I hope this finds you well'.

Format: The email plus 3 subject-line options under 6 words each. End with a single low-commitment CTA phrased as a question.

title: LinkedIn Connection & Outreach Message
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a non-salesy LinkedIn connection note and a value-first follow-up.
prompt:
Role: You are a social-selling specialist.

Task: Write LinkedIn outreach to [PROSPECT ROLE] at [COMPANY TYPE], whose pain is [PAIN], to eventually discuss [SOLUTION]. Sound human and curious, not salesy.

Format: (1) a connection note (≤300 chars) with a genuine, personalized reason; (2) a value-first follow-up after they accept (≤100 words) that gives before it asks; (3) a soft CTA. Include [PLACEHOLDERS] for personalization.

title: Discovery / Sales Call Script
author: PromptDeck
description: Builds a structured call script from opener to next-step close.
prompt:
Role: You are a sales enablement coach.

Task: Write a call script for selling [PRODUCT] to [BUYER ROLE], goal [BOOK NEXT STEP]. Consultative, not pushy, letting the buyer talk most.

Format: Sections with sample lines — Opener & rapport; Agenda-setting; Discovery questions; Value framing tied to their pain; Handling the top objection; Trial close; Clear next-step ask. Add a 3-bullet pre-call prep checklist.

title: Client Proposal / Quote Writer
author: PromptDeck
description: Drafts a persuasive, well-structured client proposal that closes deals.
prompt:
Role: You are a proposal writer for a services business.

Task: Write a proposal for [CLIENT] to deliver [SERVICE/SCOPE] solving their goal [GOAL], with pricing [PRICING] and timeline [TIMELINE]. Lead with their outcome, not your process.

Format: Sections — Executive summary (their goal & outcome); Understanding of their situation; Proposed solution & scope; Deliverables & timeline; Investment (pricing options); Why us / proof; Clear next step & acceptance. Keep it confident and skimmable.

title: Upsell & Cross-Sell Message
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes tactful upsell/cross-sell messaging that adds value for existing customers.
prompt:
Role: You are an account manager focused on expansion.

Task: Write an upsell/cross-sell message to an existing customer of [PRODUCT] who currently has [CURRENT PLAN/USE], recommending [UPGRADE/ADD-ON] because [RELEVANT BENEFIT]. Center it on their success, not our revenue.

Format: A short email (≤130 words) — acknowledge their progress, tie the upgrade to a goal they have, state the concrete benefit, and offer a low-friction next step. Include 2 subject lines.

title: Churned Customer Win-Back Email
author: PromptDeck
description: Writes a win-back email to re-engage a lapsed or churned customer.
prompt:
Role: You are a retention marketer.

Task: Write a win-back email to a churned customer of [PRODUCT] who left [REASON, if known], offering [INCENTIVE, if any]. Be warm and low-pressure; acknowledge the gap honestly.

Format: A short email with 2 subject lines — a friendly re-opener, a reminder of the value/what's new, an easy reason to return, and a single CTA. Then a one-line SMS version.

title: Quarterly Business Review (QBR) Deck Outline
author: PromptDeck
description: Structures a QBR that proves value and expands the account.
prompt:
Role: You are a customer success / account executive.

Task: Outline a QBR deck for [CUSTOMER] using [PRODUCT], covering results to date [METRICS], goals [GOALS], and an expansion opportunity [OPPORTUNITY].

Format: A slide-by-slide outline — Agenda; Wins & ROI to date; Progress vs goals; Product usage & adoption; Roadmap relevant to them; Recommendations & expansion; Action items & owners. Add a talk-track line for each slide.
